scope: Read-only Brevo workflow detail review for visible ATD lead-flow automations.
safetyBoundaries: No workflow was activated, paused, deactivated, edited, duplicated, deleted, renamed, or published.
No workflow trigger, step, delay, condition, template, suppression rule, exit rule, or re-entry setting was changed.
No contact, list, attribute, CRM record, template, campaign, form, or sender setting was changed.
No email was sent or resent by this review.
No webhook test or analytics test event was sent.
No GA4, GTM, Meta, Google Ads, Netlify, Vercel settings, GitHub issue, or Notion state was touched.
QA identity details, contact IDs, CRM IDs, email addresses, transactional IDs, auth values, cookies, tokens, API keys, and environment values are not included.

workflowDetails.expectedTemplateRange: Templates 19-23 were previously verified as present, but this UI pass did not expose template IDs used by the workflow.

settingsVisible.exitConditions: No configured exit condition was visible; only Add exit condition was visible
settingsVisible.restartConditions: No configured restart condition was visible; only Add restart condition was visible

recentLogs: A recent redacted contact was added to Tracking Audit Leads #11.
Tracking Audit Nurture step #2 email was sent.
The contact entered a 1-day wait at step #3.
activeContacts: One redacted contact was active in Tracking Audit Nurture at step #3.
One additional redacted contact was active in Tracking Audit Nurture at step #9.

workflowDetails.riskAssessment: High. The workflow is active, has already sent at least one nurture email to a recent redacted contact, and appears to lack visible exit/suppression conditions. It conflicts with earlier expectations that Tracking Audit Nurture remain inactive until approved.
workflowDetails.recommendedAction: Immediate user decision recommended: approve pausing or a deeper read-only review of email/suppression internals before any further Tracking Audit form testing or campaign traffic.

overallFindings: All five visible lead-flow workflows were active.
No workflow editor controls were used to change state.
Template IDs were not visible in the builder without opening edit panels.
No configured exit/suppression condition was visible in the settings pages sampled; only Add exit condition controls were visible.
Contact re-entry after exit appeared unchecked in the sampled settings controls.
Recent logs confirmed sent email activity for Tracking Audit, Newsletter, and General Enquiry workflows.
The active contacts view showed three redacted active contacts: two in Tracking Audit Nurture and one in General Enquiry Workflow.
highestRisk: Tracking Audit Nurture is active, has recent sent-email and active-wait evidence, and conflicts with earlier inactive-until-approved expectations.
recommendedNextStep: Ask the user whether to approve pausing Tracking Audit Nurture or conducting a deeper read-only workflow/template/suppression review before any more lead-flow testing.
